Lab3 - Cats vs Dogs Optimization¶

In [1]:
from torchvision import models
from torchvision.models import ResNet18_Weights
import time
import random
from pathlib import Path

import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.optim as optim
from torch.utils.data import DataLoader, Subset
from torchvision import datasets, transforms
from sklearn.model_selection import StratifiedKFold, ParameterGrid, train_test_split
from sklearn.metrics import confusion_matrix

plt.rcParams["figure.dpi"] = 110

def set_seed(seed=42):
    random.seed(seed)
    np.random.seed(seed)
    torch.manual_seed(seed)
    if torch.cuda.is_available():
        torch.cuda.manual_seed_all(seed)

SEED = 42
set_seed(SEED)

if not torch.cuda.is_available():
    raise RuntimeError("CUDA is not available. This lab requires NVIDIA A100 GPU.")

gpu_name = torch.cuda.get_device_name(0)
if "A100" not in gpu_name:
    raise RuntimeError(f"Expected NVIDIA A100 GPU, found: {gpu_name}")

device = torch.device("cuda")
torch.backends.cudnn.benchmark = True

print("Device:", device, "|", gpu_name)

Device: cuda | NVIDIA A100-PCIE-40GB
In [2]:
LAB_DIR = Path(".").resolve()
DATA_ZIP = LAB_DIR / "data-cat-dog.zip"
DATA_DIR = LAB_DIR / "data-cat-dog"
TRAIN_DIR = DATA_DIR / "training_data"
TEST_DIR = DATA_DIR / "testing_data"

if not DATA_DIR.exists():
    if not DATA_ZIP.exists():
        raise FileNotFoundError(f"Missing dataset zip: {DATA_ZIP}")
    import zipfile
    with zipfile.ZipFile(DATA_ZIP, "r") as zf:
        zf.extractall(LAB_DIR)
        
IMG_SIZE = 224

train_tfms = transforms.Compose(
    [
        transforms.Resize((256, 256)),
        transforms.RandomResizedCrop(IMG_SIZE, scale=(0.75, 1.0)),
        transforms.RandomHorizontalFlip(p=0.5),
        transforms.ColorJitter(brightness=0.15, contrast=0.15, saturation=0.15),
        transforms.ToTensor(),
        transforms.Normalize((0.485, 0.456, 0.406), (0.229, 0.224, 0.225)),
    ]
)

val_tfms = transforms.Compose(
    [
        transforms.Resize((IMG_SIZE, IMG_SIZE)),
        transforms.ToTensor(),
        transforms.Normalize((0.485, 0.456, 0.406), (0.229, 0.224, 0.225)),
    ]
)

full_train_aug = datasets.ImageFolder(TRAIN_DIR, transform=train_tfms)
full_train_noaug = datasets.ImageFolder(TRAIN_DIR, transform=val_tfms)
test_ds = datasets.ImageFolder(TEST_DIR, transform=val_tfms)

class_names = full_train_aug.classes
print("Classes:", class_names)
print("Train size:", len(full_train_aug), "Test size:", len(test_ds))

Classes: ['cats', 'dogs']
Train size: 25000 Test size: 400
In [3]:
TEST_SPLIT = 0.2
BATCH_SIZE = 128
NUM_WORKERS = 10
PIN_MEMORY = True

targets = np.array(full_train_aug.targets)
indices = np.arange(len(targets))

train_idx, val_idx = train_test_split(
    indices,
    test_size=TEST_SPLIT,
    stratify=targets,
    random_state=SEED,
)

train_ds = Subset(full_train_aug, train_idx)
val_ds = Subset(full_train_noaug, val_idx)

train_loader = DataLoader(
    train_ds,
    batch_size=BATCH_SIZE,
    shuffle=True,
    num_workers=NUM_WORKERS,
    pin_memory=PIN_MEMORY,
    persistent_workers=True,
    prefetch_factor=4,
)

val_loader = DataLoader(
    val_ds,
    batch_size=BATCH_SIZE,
    shuffle=False,
    num_workers=NUM_WORKERS,
    pin_memory=PIN_MEMORY,
    persistent_workers=True,
    prefetch_factor=4,
)

test_loader = DataLoader(
    test_ds,
    batch_size=BATCH_SIZE,
    shuffle=False,
    num_workers=NUM_WORKERS,
    pin_memory=PIN_MEMORY,
    persistent_workers=True,
    prefetch_factor=4,
)

Our suggested max number of worker in current system is 4, which is smaller than what this DataLoader is going to create. Please be aware that excessive worker creation might get DataLoader running slow or even freeze, lower the worker number to avoid potential slowness/freeze if necessary.

In [4]:
def get_activation(name: str):
    name = name.lower()
    if name == "relu":
        return nn.ReLU
    if name == "sigmoid":
        return nn.Sigmoid
    raise ValueError(f"Unsupported activation: {name}")


class SimpleCNN(nn.Module):
    def __init__(
        self,
        hidden_size=256,
        dropout_rate=0.3,
        activation="relu",
        train_backbone=False,
    ):
        super().__init__()

        weights = ResNet18_Weights.DEFAULT
        self.net = models.resnet18(weights=weights)

        # 先冻结预训练主干，只训练分类头，速度快且效果稳定
        for p in self.net.parameters():
            p.requires_grad = train_backbone

        in_features = self.net.fc.in_features
        Act = get_activation(activation)

        self.net.fc = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Dropout(dropout_rate),
            nn.Linear(in_features, hidden_size),
            Act(),
            nn.Dropout(dropout_rate),
            nn.Linear(hidden_size, 1),
        )

        # 分类头一定要训练
        for p in self.net.fc.parameters():
            p.requires_grad = True

    def unfreeze_layer4(self):
        # 后期微调 ResNet 最后一组残差块，进一步提升准确率
        for p in self.net.layer4.parameters():
            p.requires_grad = True
        for p in self.net.fc.parameters():
            p.requires_grad = True

    def forward(self, x):
        return self.net(x)
In [5]:
def build_optimizer(params, name, lr):
    name = name.lower()
    if name == "adam":
        return optim.Adam(params, lr=lr)
    if name == "sgd":
        return optim.SGD(params, lr=lr, momentum=0.9)
    if name == "rmsprop":
        return optim.RMSprop(params, lr=lr)
    raise ValueError(f"Unsupported optimizer: {name}")

def _sync(device):
    if device.type == "cuda":
        torch.cuda.synchronize()

def clear_cache(device):
    if device.type == "cuda":
        torch.cuda.empty_cache()

def train_one_epoch(model, loader, criterion, optimizer, device):
    model.train()
    total_loss = 0.0
    correct = 0
    total = 0

    scaler = torch.cuda.amp.GradScaler(enabled=(device.type == "cuda"))

    for x, y in loader:
        x = x.to(device, non_blocking=True)
        y = y.to(device, non_blocking=True).float().unsqueeze(1)

        optimizer.zero_grad(set_to_none=True)

        with torch.cuda.amp.autocast(enabled=(device.type == "cuda")):
            logits = model(x)
            loss = criterion(logits, y)

        scaler.scale(loss).backward()
        scaler.step(optimizer)
        scaler.update()

        total_loss += loss.item() * x.size(0)
        preds = (torch.sigmoid(logits) >= 0.5).long()
        correct += (preds == y.long()).sum().item()
        total += x.size(0)

    return total_loss / total, correct / total

@torch.no_grad()
def evaluate(model, loader, criterion, device):
    model.eval()
    total_loss = 0.0
    correct = 0
    total = 0

    for x, y in loader:
        x = x.to(device, non_blocking=True)
        y = y.to(device, non_blocking=True).float().unsqueeze(1)

        with torch.cuda.amp.autocast(enabled=(device.type == "cuda")):
            logits = model(x)
            loss = criterion(logits, y)

        total_loss += loss.item() * x.size(0)
        preds = (torch.sigmoid(logits) >= 0.5).long()
        correct += (preds == y.long()).sum().item()
        total += x.size(0)

    return total_loss / total, correct / total

def train_model(
    model,
    train_loader,
    val_loader,
    optimizer_name,
    lr,
    epochs,
    device,
 ):
    criterion = nn.BCEWithLogitsLoss()
    optimizer = build_optimizer(
    filter(lambda p: p.requires_grad, model.parameters()),
    optimizer_name,
    lr,
)

    history = {
        "epoch": [],
        "train_loss": [],
        "train_acc": [],
        "val_loss": [],
        "val_acc": [],
    }

    start = time.perf_counter()
    for epoch in range(epochs):
        train_loss, train_acc = train_one_epoch(
            model, train_loader, criterion, optimizer, device
        )
        val_loss, val_acc = evaluate(model, val_loader, criterion, device)

        history["epoch"].append(epoch + 1)
        history["train_loss"].append(train_loss)
        history["train_acc"].append(train_acc)
        history["val_loss"].append(val_loss)
        history["val_acc"].append(val_acc)

        print(
            f"Epoch {epoch + 1}/{epochs} | "
            f"train_acc={train_acc:.4f} val_acc={val_acc:.4f} "
            f"train_loss={train_loss:.4f} val_loss={val_loss:.4f}"
        )

    _sync(device)
    elapsed = time.perf_counter() - start
    return history, elapsed

Task 1 - Optimizer comparison¶

In [6]:
EPOCHS_TASK1 = 3
OPTIMIZERS = ["Adam", "SGD", "RMSprop"]
LRS = [0.0001, 0.0003, 0.001]

task1_histories = {}
task1_times = {}

for opt_name in OPTIMIZERS:
    for lr in LRS:
        print(f"\nRunning {opt_name} lr={lr}")
        model = SimpleCNN(
            hidden_size=256,
            dropout_rate=0.3,
            activation="relu",
            train_backbone=False,
        ).to(device)
        history, elapsed = train_model(
            model,
            train_loader,
            val_loader,
            optimizer_name=opt_name,
            lr=lr,
            epochs=EPOCHS_TASK1,
            device=device,
        )
        key = f"{opt_name}_lr{lr}"
        task1_histories[key] = history
        task1_times[key] = elapsed

        del model
        clear_cache(device)

In [7]:
def plot_task1_curves(histories, metric_key, title):
    plt.figure(figsize=(8, 5))
    for key, hist in histories.items():
        plt.plot(
            hist["epoch"],
            hist[metric_key],
            marker="o",
            linewidth=1.8,
            markersize=5,
            label=key,
        )
    plt.xlabel("Epoch")
    plt.ylabel(metric_key)
    plt.title(title)
    plt.legend(fontsize=8)
    plt.grid(True)
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()

plot_task1_curves(task1_histories, "train_acc", "Task1 Train Accuracy")
plot_task1_curves(task1_histories, "val_acc", "Task1 Val Accuracy")
plot_task1_curves(task1_histories, "train_loss", "Task1 Train Loss")
plot_task1_curves(task1_histories, "val_loss", "Task1 Val Loss")

plt.figure(figsize=(8, 4))
labels = list(task1_times.keys())
times = [task1_times[k] for k in labels]
plt.bar(labels, times)
plt.ylabel("Seconds")
plt.title("Task1 Training Time")
plt.xticks(rotation=45, ha="right")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
In [8]:
def summarize_convergence(histories, target_acc=0.85):
    rows = []
    for key, hist in histories.items():
        val_acc = np.array(hist["val_acc"])
        epoch_reach = next((i + 1 for i, a in enumerate(val_acc) if a >= target_acc), None)
        tail = val_acc[len(val_acc) // 2 :]
        stability = float(np.std(tail)) if len(tail) > 0 else float("nan")
        rows.append(
            {
                "run": key,
                "epoch_to_target_acc": epoch_reach,
                "val_acc_final": float(val_acc[-1]),
                "val_acc_stability_std": stability,
                "train_time_sec": float(task1_times[key]),
            }
        )
    return pd.DataFrame(rows).sort_values("val_acc_final", ascending=False)

task1_summary = summarize_convergence(task1_histories, target_acc=0.85)
task1_summary

Task1 Analysis (fill after running)¶

  • Convergence speed: compare epoch_to_target_acc and train_time_sec.
  • Stability: lower val_acc_stability_std means more stable.
  • Describe which optimizer and lr look best for this dataset.

Task 2 - Grid search with 5-fold CV¶

In [ ]:
EPOCHS_CV = 1
MAX_COMBOS = None


param_grid = {
    "optimizer": ["Adam"],
    "lr": [0.001],
    "batch_size": [512],
    "hidden_size": [128, 256],
    "dropout_rate": [0.5, 0.7],
    "activation": ["relu"],
}

grid = list(ParameterGrid(param_grid))

skf = StratifiedKFold(n_splits=5, shuffle=True, random_state=SEED)
cv_results = []

for i, params in enumerate(grid, 1):
    print(f"\n[{i}/{len(grid)}] {params}")
    fold_acc = []
    fold_loss = []

    for fold, (train_idx, val_idx) in enumerate(skf.split(indices, targets), 1):
        train_ds_fold = Subset(full_train_aug, train_idx)
        val_ds_fold = Subset(full_train_noaug, val_idx)

        train_loader_fold = DataLoader(
            train_ds_fold,
            batch_size=params["batch_size"],
            shuffle=True,
            num_workers=NUM_WORKERS,
            pin_memory=PIN_MEMORY,
            persistent_workers=True,
            prefetch_factor=4,
        )
        val_loader_fold = DataLoader(
            val_ds_fold,
            batch_size=params["batch_size"],
            shuffle=False,
            num_workers=NUM_WORKERS,
            pin_memory=PIN_MEMORY,
            persistent_workers=True,
            prefetch_factor=4,
        )

        model = SimpleCNN(
            hidden_size=params["hidden_size"],
            dropout_rate=params["dropout_rate"],
            activation=params["activation"],
            train_backbone=False,
        ).to(device)

        history, _ = train_model(
            model,
            train_loader_fold,
            val_loader_fold,
            optimizer_name=params["optimizer"],
            lr=params["lr"],
            epochs=EPOCHS_CV,
            device=device,
        )

        fold_acc.append(history["val_acc"][-1])
        fold_loss.append(history["val_loss"][-1])

        del model
        clear_cache(device)

    cv_results.append(
        {
            **params,
            "mean_val_acc": float(np.mean(fold_acc)),
            "std_val_acc": float(np.std(fold_acc)),
            "mean_val_loss": float(np.mean(fold_loss)),
        }
    )

cv_df = pd.DataFrame(cv_results).sort_values(
    ["mean_val_acc", "mean_val_loss"], ascending=[False, True]
 )
cv_df.to_csv("grid_search_results.csv", index=False)
cv_df.head(10)

In [ ]:
def param_impact(df, metric="mean_val_acc"):
    impacts = []
    for col in [
        "optimizer",
        "lr",
        "batch_size",
        "hidden_size",
        "dropout_rate",
        "activation",
    ]:
        means = df.groupby(col)[metric].mean()
        impact = means.max() - means.min()
        impacts.append({"param": col, "impact": float(impact)})
    return pd.DataFrame(impacts).sort_values("impact", ascending=False)

impact_df = param_impact(cv_df)
impact_df
Out[ ]:
param impact
4 dropout_rate 0.00182
3 hidden_size 0.00102
1 lr 0.00000
0 optimizer 0.00000

In [ ]:
best_params = cv_df.iloc[0].to_dict()

best_model = SimpleCNN(
    hidden_size=int(best_params["hidden_size"]),
    dropout_rate=float(best_params["dropout_rate"]),
    activation=best_params["activation"],
    train_backbone=False,
).to(device)

final_train_loader = DataLoader(
    full_train_aug,
    batch_size=int(best_params["batch_size"]),
    shuffle=True,
    num_workers=NUM_WORKERS,
    pin_memory=PIN_MEMORY,
    persistent_workers=True,
    prefetch_factor=4,
)

final_val_loader = DataLoader(
    full_train_noaug,
    batch_size=int(best_params["batch_size"]),
    shuffle=False,
    num_workers=NUM_WORKERS,
    pin_memory=PIN_MEMORY,
    persistent_workers=True,
    prefetch_factor=4,
)

# 第一阶段：冻结 ResNet 主干，只训练分类头
print("Stage 1: train classifier head")
history_head, time_head = train_model(
    best_model,
    final_train_loader,
    final_val_loader,
    optimizer_name="Adam",
    lr=float(best_params["lr"]),
    epochs=5,
    device=device,
)

# 第二阶段：解冻 ResNet 最后一层 layer4，低学习率微调
print("Stage 2: fine-tune layer4")
best_model.unfreeze_layer4()

history_ft, time_ft = train_model(
    best_model,
    final_train_loader,
    final_val_loader,
    optimizer_name="Adam",
    lr=1e-5,
    epochs=3,
    device=device,
)

test_loss, test_acc = evaluate(
    best_model,
    test_loader,
    nn.BCEWithLogitsLoss(),
    device,
)

print(f"Test loss: {test_loss:.4f} | Test acc: {test_acc:.4f}")

Task 3 - Visualization and error analysis¶

In [ ]:
from PIL import Image

class ImageFolderWithPaths(datasets.ImageFolder):
    def __getitem__(self, index):
        img, label = super().__getitem__(index)
        path = self.samples[index][0]
        return img, label, path

test_ds_paths = ImageFolderWithPaths(TEST_DIR, transform=val_tfms)
test_loader_paths = DataLoader(
    test_ds_paths,
    batch_size=BATCH_SIZE,
    shuffle=False,
    num_workers=NUM_WORKERS,
    pin_memory=False,
)

@torch.no_grad()
def predict_all(model, loader, device):
    model.eval()
    probs = []
    labels = []
    paths = []
    for x, y, p in loader:
        x = x.to(device)
        logits = model(x)
        prob = torch.sigmoid(logits).squeeze(1).cpu().numpy()
        probs.extend(prob.tolist())
        labels.extend(y.numpy().tolist())
        paths.extend(p)
    return np.array(probs), np.array(labels), np.array(paths)

probs, labels, paths = predict_all(best_model, test_loader_paths, device)
preds = (probs >= 0.5).astype(int)

cm = confusion_matrix(labels, preds)
plt.figure(figsize=(4, 4))
plt.imshow(cm, cmap="Blues")
plt.title("Confusion Matrix")
plt.xlabel("Predicted")
plt.ylabel("True")
plt.xticks([0, 1], class_names)
plt.yticks([0, 1], class_names)
for (i, j), v in np.ndenumerate(cm):
    plt.text(j, i, int(v), ha="center", va="center", color="black")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
In [ ]:
def show_samples(sample_indices, title):
    plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))
    for i, idx in enumerate(sample_indices, 1):
        img = Image.open(paths[idx]).convert("RGB")
        plt.subplot(1, len(sample_indices), i)
        plt.imshow(img)
        prob = probs[idx]
        pred = class_names[int(prob >= 0.5)]
        true = class_names[labels[idx]]
        plt.title(f"pred={pred}\ntrue={true}\nprob={prob:.2f}")
        plt.axis("off")
    plt.suptitle(title)
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()

rand_indices = random.sample(range(len(paths)), 5)
show_samples(rand_indices, "Random Test Samples")
In [ ]:
hard_indices = np.argsort(np.abs(probs - 0.5))[:5]
show_samples(hard_indices.tolist(), "Hardest Samples (prob near 0.5)")

Task3 Analysis (fill after running)¶

  • Inspect the hardest samples and describe why they are ambiguous.
  • Check which class is confused more often in the confusion matrix.
